Home»Lifestyle»Celeb News»International Celebs»BTS’ Jin shines as Paris Olympics torchbearer

Jin, a member of BTS, carried the torch for the 2024 Paris Summer Olympics at the Louvre Museum, delighting fans who gathered to see him.

Jin, a beloved member of the global K-pop sensation BTS, captivated Paris on Sunday as he carried the torch for the 2024 Paris Summer Olympics.

Greeted by a sea of adoring fans, Jin led the relay at the iconic Louvre Museum, making it a memorable evening for all.

The event began around 8 p.m., with crowds of BTS fans, affectionately known as ARMY, gathering to catch a glimpse of their favourite star.

Signs reading “Welcome, Seok-jin, I Love You,” “Run, Seok-jin,” and “Good luck, Seok-jin” adorned the area. This was alongside fluttering Korean national flags.

Jin, whose birth name is Kim Seok-jin, was met with overwhelming support, according toBillboard.

As Jin emerged from the torch relay centre, the crowd erupted in cheers, chanting his name.

With a shy smile, he waved to his fans, acknowledging their love and support.

Escorted by police, Jin then moved to Rue de Rivoli, where he was handed the torch.

He ran about 200 meters before returning it to the relay centre, according to theDaily Guardian.

The event coincided with Bastille Day, adding to the festive atmosphere.

“I feel extremely honoured to have been able to participate in today’s torch relay,” Jin said afterwards. This was according to Hybe, the parent company of his agency, BigHit Music.

“I sincerely thank ARMY and all the fans who made it possible for me to carry the torch.”

Jin, now 31, recently completed his mandatory military service, becoming the first BTS member to do so.

Fans are excited because they expect him to focus on solo activities in the coming months.

The French domestic torch relay, which began on 8 May in Marseille, has been underway for over two months, according to theKorea Times.

The Olympic torch, which arrived in Paris on Sunday, will travel through various parts of the French capital until Monday before returning to the provinces.

It will then come back to Paris for the grand opening ceremony on 26 July.

The excitement among fans was palpable.

Marie, a dedicated BTS fan who travelled from Lyon to witness the event, said, “Seeing Jin carry the torch was a dream come true.”

“His smile lit up the whole street.”

Another fan, Emma, held a sign that read, “Run, Seok-jin.”

She shared, “I’ve followed BTS for years, and seeing Jin here in Paris, representing the Olympics, is incredible. It’s a moment I’ll never forget.”

Jin’s participation in the torch relay is a testament to his global influence and the power of BTS’s music and message.

The group, known for their positive and uplifting songs, has a massive following worldwide.

Jin’s appearance in Paris not only thrilled fans but also showcased the unifying power of music and sport.

As the torch relay continues, anticipation builds for the opening ceremony of the 2024 Paris Summer Olympics.

Jin’srolein the relay will undoubtedly be remembered as one of its highlights.

His humble and heartfelt presence left a lasting impression on all who witnessed it.

In the words of one fan, “Jin running with the torch is a symbol of hope and unity.

It shows that no matter where we come from, we can come together to celebrate something beautiful.”

With the Olympics just around the corner, Jin’s contribution to the torch relay has set a high bar.

His performance was not just a run, but a moment of connection and joy for fans around the world.

His participation highlighted the intersection of pop culture and international sport, bringing immense visibility to the Olympics among younger generations.

Fans worldwide felt a sense of pride and connection, as Jin’s involvement symbolised unity and perseverance. What did you think?

description: Jin, a member of BTS, carried the torch for the 2024 Paris Summer Olympics at the Louvre Museum, delighting fans who gathered to see him.
